moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Buy XLV at 10% to diversify into a new sector, reducing tech concentration.
The remaining CRWD and SMH will be held at their current reduced percentages.
This plan aims for capital preservation by significantly de-risking the portfolio.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% monthly, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns. However, this also limits upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL to remove the highest-risk leveraged ETF, while keeping a small SPAL position.
Trim QQQI to 15% due to its downtrend, reallocating to stronger broad index.
Increase QQQ to 20% and CRWD to 15% to lean into quality tech and broad market exposure.
Buy RTX at 8% to diversify the portfolio with a high-scoring new setup.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% monthly, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while still participating in market upside. Expected drawdowns are moderate, but market corrections can still impact returns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Maintain SOXL at 5% and increase SPAL to 5% to lean into leveraged ETFs.
Sell QQQI and trim SMH to exit downtrending positions, freeing capital.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press a winning AI position with strong momentum.
Buy RTX at 10% and AMAT at 10% to add high-scoring, strong setups.
This plan uses leverage and concentration to target higher growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly, aiming for stretched growth. It leverages high-scoring setups and uses leveraged ETFs, which significantly increases both upside and potential drawdown. Be aware that leveraged ETFs decay daily, and a sudden market reversal could lead to substantial losses very quickly, requiring strict stop discipline.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% and QQQ to 10% to reduce growth exposure and free capital for income.
Sell all SOXL, SPAL, CRWD, and SMH to eliminate leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Increase QQQI to 30% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Buy XLV at 10% to diversify into a lower-beta sector for stability.
This plan aims to park cash for yield, focusing on low drawdown and reliable income.
A significant cash buffer will be maintained to further reduce risk.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% monthly, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable monthly distributions from income ETFs. While aiming for stability, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff, meaning this is not a savings account and some capital risk remains.
